summary: Large Shrine Route stage decompilation PR. It replaced several grshrineroute.c placeholder stubs with C, improved the Shrine Route ground variable layouts in src/melee/gr/types.h, and tightened grshrineroute.h prototypes. The decomp.dev bot reported 6 new full matches and 7 partial-match improvements, raising matched code by +1980 bytes. Most work centered on Shrine Route state-machine logic, route symbols, platforms, camera tracking, materials, lighting, effects, player callbacks, and mpLib joint/collision interaction.

key_files.role: Main implementation work for Shrine Route functions, including route symbol setup, stage initialization, route interaction state machine, light setup, platform/camera handling, and helper callbacks.

Refined grShrineroute_GroundVars, grShrineroute_GroundVars2, and grShrineroute_GroundVars3 layouts to expose timers, bitmasks, object arrays, platform offsets, light arrays, and motion fields used by the C implementation.
Replaced UNK prototypes with typed signatures for fn_80208A38(HSD_GObj*) and grShrineRoute_8020AE08(HSD_GObj*, HSD_GObj*, s32*).

what_changed: Implemented fn_80208A38 as a Shrine Route symbol-spawn callback: it picks one of six flags with HSD_Randi, creates symbol ground/effect objects via grShrineRoute_802088C0, positions them from Ground_801C2CF4/lb_8000B1CC, and applies configured scale.

Implemented grShrineRoute_80208D14 initialization: starts map animation, clears callbacks, initializes shrineroute state fields, clears symbol slots, configures ftCo_800C07F8 and mpJointSetCb1 callbacks, stores moving-platform JObj offsets relative to a center point, and schedules fn_80208A38 through Ground_801C10B8.

Added a large near-match implementation for grShrineRoute_80208F70, covering the Shrine Route route-interaction state machine: detecting route triggers, hiding/revealing joints, disabling/enabling mp joints, spawning/removing route symbols, updating stage_info.x6DC, driving animations/material changes, invoking Zako generator calls, invoking player/game callbacks, camera target smoothing, and moving platform tracking.

Implemented or improved additional Shrine Route helpers including grShrineRoute_8020A8A4, grShrineRoute_8020AA40, grShrineRoute_8020AB58, grShrineRoute_8020AE08, grShrineRoute_8020AF38, and grShrineRoute_8020B020.

Added explicit externs and a local grSh_Route_LightConfig structure for light data such as grSh_Route_803E5D74, grSh_Route_803B8360, grSh_Route_803B836C, grSh_Route_803B8384, grSh_Route_803B8390, and grSh_Route_803E5A58.
Adjusted a static stage-data struct so fields x1C and x20 are f32 instead of int, matching their use as scaled radius/distance values.
Expanded includes for camera, game, ground animation/lib/stage, ftdevice, lbvector, math_ppc, trigf, and other dependencies needed by the newly decompiled code.

Changed grShrineroute_GroundVars from sparse/incorrect fields into a documented layout with xC4 state, xC6 completion bitmask, xC8 selected route id, xCE/xD0 timers, xD4 effect object pointer, platforms[3], and symbols[6].
Changed grShrineroute_GroundVars2 to model arrays of HSD_LObj* and u32 values at xC8 and x118 instead of opaque padding and single fields.
Extended grShrineroute_GroundVars3 with additional f32 fields xD8, xDC, xE0 and HSD_JObj* xE4.
decomp.dev reported new 100% matches for grShrineRoute_80208D14, grShrineRoute_8020A8A4, grShrineRoute_8020AE08, grShrineRoute_8020AA40, grShrineRoute_8020AB58, and grShrineRoute_80209AF0.
smart_moves: The struct-layout work in types.h made the main state-machine implementation much more natural: symbols[6] replaced repeated opaque access, platforms[3] captured the offset/JObj pair used by platform tracking, and xC6 was exposed as the bitmask for completed routes.

The implementation preserved matching-sensitive artifacts where needed, including PAD_STACK uses, unused local floats, explicit casts, and exact-looking constants such as 0xBD route IDs, 0x3C timers, 30.0f camera/platform offsets, and small radian velocity limits.

Separating grSh_Route_LightConfig from raw extern data allowed grShrineRoute_8020AA40 to express color, position, distance attenuation, interest, spot, and flags setup cleanly while keeping field names offset-based due to weak semantic evidence.
Typed header prototypes removed UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for functions whose call signatures were now understood, improving future compile-time checking and making later stage work easier to call safely.
The route state machine was kept evidence-grounded with offset names like xC4, xC6, xC8, xCE, and xD4 rather than inventing semantic names prematurely.
A tiny inline wrapper grShrineRoute_8020A8A4_rand returning HSD_Randf appears to have been used as a matching tactic for random-based expression/codegen behavior.
decomp_lessons: For stage files, correct Ground->gv union layouts are often the unlock for large decompilation chunks; replacing padding with arrays and nested structs can turn hard pointer arithmetic into readable C and expose state-machine structure.

When a stage state machine uses map IDs or joint IDs as externally meaningful constants, preserve the raw constants until names are justified. This PR keeps IDs such as 0xBD through 0xC2 and joint indices 8 through 0xD visible.

Camera-follow code may need to combine player position, stage camera target helpers, timer-based interpolation, and special platform-tracking modes; matching such code benefits from explicit Vec3 temporaries and lbVector_Add/Sub calls.
Callback signatures matter: ftCo_800C07F8 and mpJointSetCb1 usage helped establish grShrineRoute_8020AE08 and grShrineRoute_8020AD58 types.
For lighting decompilation, model the data table first. A table-backed config struct can make repeated HSD_LObjSetColor/Position/DistAttn/Spot calls straightforward while preserving exact offsets.
Use typed arrays in structs when repeated operations reveal cardinality, such as six route symbols, three platform trackers, and twenty light/object entries in Shrine Route ground vars.
Do not rename offset fields too aggressively when behavior is only partially understood; offset names remain searchable and safer for future refinements.
naming_conventions: Stage functions retain the project’s address-based naming style: grShrineRoute_80208D14, grShrineRoute_80208F70, grShrineRoute_8020AE08, etc.
Internal callback keeps fn_80208A38 naming because no semantic name was proven.
Ground variable structs use grShrineroute_GroundVars, grShrineroute_GroundVars2, and grShrineroute_GroundVars3 with offset-style field names such as xC4, xC6, xC8, xCE, xD4.
New nested fields use meaningful names only where evidence is strong: platforms[3].offset, platforms[3].jobj, and symbols[6].
External data symbols keep address-based names like grSh_Route_803E5A58 and grSh_Route_803B8384.
The local light data struct is named grSh_Route_LightConfig but its members remain offset-based x0, x4, x10, x1C, etc.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK in several newly implemented functions, including fn_80208A38, grShrineRoute_80208D14, grShrineRoute_80208F70, and grShrineRoute_8020AB58, indicating stack-shape preservation for matching.
Kept unused float locals in grShrineRoute_8020AE08 and fn_80208A38, likely to maintain stack/register behavior.
Used an inline HSD_Randf wrapper in grShrineRoute_8020A8A4_rand, apparently to influence random-call codegen in grShrineRoute_8020A8A4.
Used explicit casts between HSD_GObj*, Ground_GObj*, Ground*, HSD_JObj*, and callback types to match existing engine APIs and function-pointer expectations.
Preserved literal constants and exact float constants, including 6.283185307179586, 0.04363323f, 0.0008726646f, 0.99f, 0.7f, 300.0f, and 30.0f.
Reworked grShrineRoute_8020B020 from a suspicious single x108 object field into iteration over gp->gv.shrineroute.symbols[i], removing an odd gp reassignment at loop end.

review_feedback.feedback: Only says "Tracking PR as usual"; no technical rationale was provided by the author.
Reported matched code improved to 62.06%, +0.05%, +1980 bytes, with 6 new matches and 7 partial improvements.

New full matches listed: grShrineRoute_80208D14 (+504), grShrineRoute_8020A8A4 (+412), grShrineRoute_8020AE08 (+304), grShrineRoute_8020AA40 (+280), grShrineRoute_8020AB58 (+236), and grShrineRoute_80209AF0 (+1).
Partial improvements included grShrineRoute_80208F70 at 98.56%, grShrineRoute_8020A21C at 91.29%, grShrineRoute_80209BEC at 97.05%, fn_80208A38 at 97.84%, and grShrineRoute_8020AF38 at 76.97%.
No human review comments were present in the slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish grShrineRoute_80208F70 from 98.56% to 100%.
Find later PRs that rename grShrineroute_GroundVars fields xC4, xC6, xC8, xCE, or xD4 with semantic names.
Search for other stage PRs using typed symbols arrays or platform offset arrays in GroundVars.
Search for grSh_Route_LightConfig or HSD_LObj light table decomp patterns in other ground/stage files.
Look for follow-up work on grShrineRoute_8020A21C, grShrineRoute_80209BEC, fn_80208A38, or grShrineRoute_8020AF38 partial matches.
